argument_objects.statement: Documents are only one projection of professional knowledge; recurring relationships among people, tasks, tools, exceptions, choices, rationales, and outcomes can encode valuable operating and judgment patterns.
argument_objects.title: The brain trust is an interaction graph

argument_objects.statement: If many firms authorize retention or generalized improvement from overlapping professional interaction data, each contribution may strengthen a shared upstream capability whose benefits and competitive effects extend beyond the contributing firm.
argument_objects.title: Peer contribution can create a sector externality

argument_objects.statement: AlphaGo's Move 37 demonstrates that learned evaluation and search in a closed strategic domain can select an unconventional action whose value becomes clear much later; it motivates testing for analogous delayed-value legal strategies.
argument_objects.title: Move-37-like strategic discontinuity is possible but unproven in law

argument_objects.statement: The provider-mediated sector-externality mechanism is weakened for a bounded deployment if independent contractual and technical evidence proves that ordinary and optional paths prevent retention, cross-customer use, generalized improvement, and semantic derivative reuse.
argument_objects.title: Verified isolation and no generalized derivation

argument_objects.statement: The overlap thesis weakens if reproducible experiments show that multi-firm interaction abstractions do not improve legal strategy, workflow, or evaluation performance over public-only and single-firm baselines.
argument_objects.title: No measurable substitution from multi-firm traces

argument_objects.statement: The existential competitive framing weakens if legal strategy capability plateaus despite additional compute, algorithms, tools, public data, and synthetic experience, or remains dependent on non-transferable human context.
argument_objects.title: Frontier legal strategy plateaus

argument_objects.statement: The repository has no representative measure of how much operational and professional-judgment ontology overlaps across large law firms or how much unique alpha remains after normalization.
argument_objects.title: Law-firm ontology overlap is unmeasured

argument_objects.statement: No registered evidence establishes that a named provider aggregates ordinary law-firm interaction patterns across customers for generalized model, product, or strategy improvement.
argument_objects.title: Provider aggregation and use are unproven

argument_objects.statement: There is no registered benchmark that reliably identifies unconventional, delayed-value legal strategies while measuring legal correctness, duties, client objectives, institutional legitimacy, and downstream consequences.
argument_objects.title: No validated legal Move 37 benchmark

argument_objects.statement: The public record reviewed does not establish that Anthropic or another named frontier provider covertly pools identifiable law-firm ontologies or trains on ordinary commercial content by default.
argument_objects.title: No evidence of current covert pooling

argument_objects.statement: Firm-controlled AI can improve quality, speed, access, consistency, training, and strategic experimentation, so external AI use need not flatten law firms or transfer their control plane.
argument_objects.title: AI can complement and differentiate firms

argument_objects.statement: Statutes, opinions, public filings, forms, professional rules, and widely used workflows limit the portion of legal knowledge that any firm can claim as unique or defensibly withhold.
argument_objects.title: The common legal layer is not proprietary

argument_objects.statement: Go has fixed rules, a complete visible state, cheap self-play, and a clean objective; law is open, institutional, normative, fact-dependent, relationship-dependent, and governed by professional duties.
argument_objects.title: Go is not law

argument_objects.statement: AlphaGo Zero demonstrates that a system in a closed strategic domain can surpass expert-trained predecessors through self-play without human game data, so withholding private expert examples may deny a shortcut without preventing formidable capability.
argument_objects.title: Private expert traces may be unnecessary

argument_objects.statement: The Ajax Line does not reject AI; it calls for firm-controlled ontology, evaluation, rights, telemetry, portability, and audit so firms can obtain benefits without granting an unexamined upstream learning right.
argument_objects.title: Precautionary sovereignty is compatible with adoption

argument_objects.statement: Each major law firm should independently maintain a defensive boundary around its collective attorney brain-trust data and derived representations because peer exposure could create an upstream capability that reduces overlapping differentiation, including for protective holdouts.
argument_objects.title: The Ajax Line: hold the line

claims.does_not_prove: Provider-side retention, generalized training, covert reuse, or intent.

evidence.locator: Product sections on playbooks, standards, fallbacks, setup, and risk calibration
Cold-start interviews; practice profiles; deal-debrief; playbook-monitor
Captured sections on legal plugins, playbooks, precedent, and house style
Captured sections on skills, subagents, playbooks, precedent, and house style

claims.proposition: The pinned repository and captured official product pages document a static design that invites legal users to encode playbooks, fallbacks, deviations, rationales, risk posture, and house style.

claims.does_not_prove: A hosted runtime never receives profile content through inference or a separate upload.
claims.proposition: The official repository does not automatically upload a populated local practice profile, matter folder, deviation log, or proposal history through its Managed Agent deployment script.

claims.does_not_prove: Technical compliance, zero processing, zero retention, or the terms of an executed customer agreement.
claims.proposition: Anthropic's public standard commercial terms prohibit model training on Customer Content by default and leave input/output ownership with the customer.

claims.does_not_prove: Retained material is used for generalized training or cross-customer productization.
claims.proposition: Registered official documentation describes feature-dependent retention and zero-data-retention policy boundaries for hosted services.

claims.does_not_prove: Ordinary commercial conversations are used by default or that raw connector content is always included.
claims.proposition: Commercial feedback submission is a disclosed secondary-use path that can include the related conversation and support research, behavior analysis, and model training.

claims.does_not_prove: Measured exit cost, anticompetitive conduct, customer harm, or malicious intent.
claims.proposition: Deep integration of firm playbooks, connectors, shared Skills, recurring agents, governance, and user habits can create dependency and switching costs without transferring ownership or proving covert training.

claims.does_not_prove: That implementation is flawless or that closed runtime, support, telemetry, negotiated agreements, and future practices pose no risk.
claims.proposition: No evidence currently registered in this project establishes covert pooling of identifiable law-firm ontologies or default training on ordinary commercial content.

claims.does_not_prove: That AlphaGo's strategy transfers to law, that the move was creative in a human sense, or that a legal system can optimize an equivalent reward.

evidence.locator: A creative spark section, captured page lines 140-144

claims.proposition: AlphaGo's famous Move 37 occurred in Game 2 against Lee Sedol; professional commentators initially thought the unconventional move was a mistake, and DeepMind's retrospective describes it as decisive much later in the game.

claims.does_not_prove: That law has an adequate rule set or reward for self-play, or that private legal expertise is unnecessary for every useful legal capability.

evidence.locator: Abstract, page lines 64-66 in the 2026-07-14 observation

claims.proposition: AlphaGo Zero achieved superhuman Go performance using reinforcement learning from self-play without human game data, guidance, or domain knowledge beyond the rules.

claims.does_not_prove: Indefinite exponential improvement, a universal efficiency law, or any forecast of frontier legal-strategy capability.
claims.proposition: A NeurIPS 2024 study found substantial historical algorithmic-efficiency progress across more than 200 language-model evaluations from 2012 through 2023, while attributing more overall performance improvement to increased compute than to algorithmic progress.

claims.does_not_prove: That these assets are legally owned, technically learnable by a frontier model, transferable outside the firm, or sufficient to reproduce attorney judgment.
claims.proposition: Organizational research treats knowledge embedded in relationships among people, tasks, tools, business processes, and employee skills as a potential basis for competitive advantage; empirical law-firm research describes law firms as knowledge-intensive organizations.

claims.does_not_prove: Cross-firm ontology overlap, technical learnability, substitutability, provider rights or aggregation, or competitive harm to a law firm; each remains unmeasured.

evidence.locator: Sections on customer-held and specialised industry data, feedback effects, concentration, and market tipping

claims.proposition: If multiple firms authorize an upstream AI supplier to retain or generalize substantially overlapping professional interaction patterns, the resulting capability could create a sector externality that reduces the relative scarcity of overlapping knowledge even for a firm that withholds its own data.

claims.does_not_prove: That a specific standards effort or firm decision violates antitrust law; legal analysis remains fact-specific and requires counsel.

evidence.locator: Opening explanation, health-provider example, and purchasing-manager Q&A

claims.proposition: FTC guidance distinguishes a business's unilateral choice of trading partners from competitor agreements not to deal with a targeted supplier or to deal only on agreed terms, which may constitute an unlawful group boycott.

claims.does_not_prove: That deployed behavior is unsafe, safe, equivalent to the repository, or different from the repository.
claims.proposition: Whether the static repository design maps to a deployed legal-product runtime, and how that runtime moves or retains tenant data, remains unknown in the registered evidence.

claims.does_not_prove: Any tenant's actual configuration, data movement, retention, training use, breach, or compliance failure.

evidence.locator: Feature-dependent retention and ZDR policy boundary
Customer-specific processing instructions and agreement boundary

claims.proposition: A particular tenant's negotiated terms, enabled features, retention settings, connector behavior, and operational compliance remain unknown in the registered evidence.

thesis_nodes.does_not_prove: Ownership, legal protectability, uniqueness, portability, technical learnability, or economic value.

evidence_paths.limitation: Organizational-learning research is not direct evidence of technical learnability or legal ownership.
A 2000 Norwegian law-firm study does not establish current frontier-model effects.

thesis_nodes.proposition: Firm-specific value can reside in context-to-choice-to-rationale-to-outcome relationships, not merely in standalone documents.

thesis_nodes.does_not_prove: Actual tenant configuration, data movement, retention, training, connector execution, or deployed runtime equivalence.

evidence_paths.limitation: This exact deployment-script path establishes only a negative static-code observation.
A captured product announcement describes intended design, not a deployed tenant runtime.

thesis_nodes.proposition: Pinned legal materials invite users to encode playbooks, fallbacks, deviations, rationales, risk posture, precedent, and house style.

thesis_nodes.does_not_prove: That a named provider currently captures, retains, pools, trains on, or generalizes a firm's ontology.

evidence_paths.limitation: Documented retention policy is not verified tenant behavior or a source-to-sink runtime trace.
Standard commercial terms are counterevidence but do not prove technical compliance or a negotiated agreement.

thesis_nodes.proposition: Connectors, feedback, support, telemetry, or hosted features could expose professional signals if material leaves firm custody and reuse is permitted.

thesis_nodes.does_not_prove: Present harm, provider intent, data aggregation, antitrust violation, transferability, substitutability, or inevitability.

evidence_paths.limitation: Competition-policy pathways and organizational theory do not establish actual cross-firm overlap or substitution.

thesis_nodes.proposition: If overlapping multi-firm professional traces are legally and technically reusable, resulting capability could reduce the scarcity of firm-specific capability.

thesis_nodes.does_not_prove: Guaranteed security, competitive superiority, compliance, provider wrongdoing, or business survival.

evidence_paths.limitation: General FTC guidance requires fact-specific legal analysis and does not bless coordinated vendor action.
A normative rebuttal is a proposed governance position, not empirical proof of effectiveness.

thesis_nodes.proposition: Each firm should independently control ontology, derivatives, telemetry, evaluation, portability, and exit while preserving lawful unilateral decision-making.

thesis_nodes.does_not_prove: That an AlphaGo moment exists in law, that Go predicts legal performance, or that private expert traces are necessary.

evidence_paths.limitation: A first-party retrospective about a closed game cannot establish an analogous legal capability.

thesis_nodes.proposition: Legal-AI research should test unconventional delayed-value strategies, while treating Move 37 as analogy and benchmark motivation only.

thesis_nodes.does_not_prove: Impossibility, flawless compliance, zero processing or retention, absence of negotiated differences, or harmlessness.

evidence_paths.limitation: A bounded registry absence cannot establish impossibility or describe evidence outside this project.
Contractual counterevidence does not prove zero retention, flawless compliance, or harmlessness.

thesis_nodes.proposition: No evidence currently registered in this project establishes covert pooling of identifiable firm ontologies or default training on ordinary commercial content.
